title: Status Page Update Cadence During Major Incident

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST CSF RC.CO-02 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST CSF RC.CO-02 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.7.50: Status Page Update Cadence During Major Incident.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.7.50' running on statuspage:incident, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=comms sourcetype=statuspage:incident earliest=-180d
| stats count as updates, earliest(_time) as start by incident_id
| eval duration_h=(now()-start)/3600
| where duration_h>1 AND updates<2
description: Ingests statuspage.io updates to prove recovery communications cadence met customer commitments.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes comms and sourcetypes statuspage:incident. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for Status Page Update Cadence During Major Incident. The NIST Cybersecurity Framework tells us what good practice looks like, and we keep evidence we match it.
implementation: (1) Map to customer SLAs; (2) training for incident comms role; (3) post-mortem metric. Indexes required: `index=comms`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=statuspage:incident`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: HEC `_json`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `statuspage:incident`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map to customer SLAs; (2) training for incident comms role; (3) post-mortem metric.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=comms sourcetype=statuspage:incident earliest=-180d
| stats count as updates, earliest(_time) as start by incident_id
| eval duration_h=(now()-start)/3600
| where duration_h>1 AND updates<2
```

Understanding this SPL

**Status Page Update Cadence During Major Incident (RC.CO-02)** — Ingests statuspage.io updates to prove recovery communications cadence met customer commitments.

Documented **Data sources**: `statuspage:incident`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): HEC `_json`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: comms; **sourcetype**: statuspage:incident. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=comms, sourcetype=statuspage:incident, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by incident_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **duration_h** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where duration_h>1 AND updates<2` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Single value
knownFalsePositives: Recovery time variance from incident complexity, dependency cascades, or limited spare capacity.
